data.title: OGE Energy declares $0.4213 dividend
data.description:
• OGE Energy (NYSE:OGE) declares $0.4213/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.84%
  • Payable April 25; for shareholders of record April 7; ex-div April 7.
  • See OGE Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Predictive Oncology announces registered direct offering; shares fall
data.description:
• Predictive Oncology (NASDAQ:POAI) has entered into definitive agreements for the purchase and sale of 363,336 shares of common stock at $1.50/share in a registered direct offering.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about February 19, 2025.
  • The gross proceeds to the company from the offering are expected to be $545,004.
  • POAI intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • POAI shares slump near 16% premarket on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: VanadiumCorp reduces debt by 80% through share issuance for management fees
data.description:
• VanadiumCorp Resource (TSXV:VRB:CA) announced a significant reduction in carried debt from outstanding management fees.
  • The company will be issuing shares for this debt.
  • VanadiumCorp has reached an agreement with eight of ten current and former management and directors to accept shares in exchange for fees owed to them.
  • The parties to the debt settlement have agreed to reduce the original amount of cash owing $458,606 to $91,722; a reduction of 80%.
  • An amount of $258,286 remains owing to former consultants and directors.
  • A total of 733,776 shares will be issued at $0.125.
  • The shares will be subject to a four-month hold period.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Nixxy expects to complete an acquisition by month-end, withdraws BTC financing
data.description:
• Nixxy (NASDAQ:NIXX) said on Wednesday that it has withdrawn a proposed private offering of up to $50M aggregate principal amount of Bitcoin-based, zero-coupon convertible notes.
  • The company continues to evaluate its financing options; however, the board has determined that with Nixxy's current significant progress in its acquisition strategy, combined with less than favorable market conditions, such financing is no longer in the shareholders' best interest.
  • The company's previously discussed business plan to locate an accretive, revenue-generating business or assets for acquisition has come to fruition much more quickly than anticipated.
  • The company is currently concluding an active transaction negotiation with entities that have generated material revenues and show the promise of substantial growth. The company expects, but cannot guarantee, closing an acquisition before the end of February.
  • Nixxy (NASDAQ:NIXX) shares were up more than 9% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Wingstop slides after same-store sales growth misses high-flying expectations
data.description:
Wingstop (NASDAQ:WING) fell in early trading on Wednesday after missing sales estimates with its Q4 earnings report. System-wide sales rose 27.6% year-over-year to $1.2 billion during the quarter, helped by 105 net new openings compared to a year ago.

Domestic same-store sales were 10.1% higher year-over-year, against a tough comparable from a year ago of +21.2%. That mark also missed the consensus expectation of +11.8%. Domestic AUV was $2.14 million in Q4, vs. $1.83 million a year ago.

As a percentage of company-owned restaurant sales, cost of sales increased to 77.6% from 75.1% a year ago. The increase was driven by food, beverage and packaging costs, primarily resulting from an increase in the cost of bone-in chicken wings. Wingstop (NASDAQ:WING) highlighted that purchases in the prior fiscal fourth quarter were tied primarily to the spot market, which benefited from significant deflation in the cost of bone-in chicken wings.

Adjusted EBITDA increased to $56.4 million from $39.1 million a year ago. GAAP EPS was $0.92 vs. $0.87 consensus and $0.64 a year ago.

There were 2,563 Wingstop restaurants system-wide at the end of Q4. The restaurant tally included 2,204 restaurants in the United States, of which 2,154 were franchised restaurants and 50 were company-owned, and 359 franchised restaurants were in international markets and U.S. territories.

"Due to the strength and staying power of our multi-year strategies, we believe we have line of sight to a new AUV target of $3.0 million," highlighted CEO Michael Skipworth. "This growth in AUVs has further enhanced our best-in-class unit economics, and as we continue to open new restaurants at a record pace, we believe there is an opportunity to more than triple our current U.S. footprint," he added.

Looking ahead, Wingstop (WING) expects low- to mid-single digit domestic same store sales growth in 2025. Global unit growth of 14% to 15% is anticipated.

Shares of Wingstop (WING) were down 7.9% in premarket action to $282.00 vs. the 52-week range of $266.45 to $433.86.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures inch lower, day after S&P 500 touches record high
data.description:
Stock index futures inched lower on Wednesday, a day after the S&P 500 notched up a record high, while investors assessed more tariff plans of U.S. President Donald Trump.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.57%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.31%.

The S&P 500 finished at a closing high Tuesday after investors spent a choppy session with their attention on geopolitics and potential deal-making.

"Talks over Ukraine were an important catalyst for yesterday’s advance as hopes for an end to the conflict helped power the European advance, where equities have seen a clear outperformance against their global counterparts so far this year," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Trump's trade policies continued to dominate the headlines, as he said he would probably announce 25% tariffs on imports of cars, semiconductors, and pharmaceuticals on April 2.

"Trump rapidly retreated from trade taxes that would be very visible to US consumers—Gen Z would notice tariff-induced inflation for avocado on toast. Less visible taxes (like aluminum) have endured," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The new proposals are partially visible—taxing new cars raises second-hand car prices and the cost of car insurance—but probably sufficiently disguised as to prevent a repeat retreat, Donovan added.

Housing starts dropped more than forecast in January, while building permits unexpectedly ticked up.

On the economic side, the Atlanta Fed's business inflation expectations report is due on Wednesday.

The FOMC meeting minutes are due later in the day.

data.title: Asset Entities regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• Asset Entities (NASDAQ:ASST) said it has regained compliance with Nasdaq's rule, which requires minimum stockholders’ equity of $2.5 million.
  • Press Release

data.title: DocGo expands into Chattanooga region in collaboration with Tennessee healthcare system
data.description:
• DocGo (NASDAQ:DCGO) said on Wednesday that its Ambulnz subsidiary has extended its contract with a major Tennessee healthcare system to launch services in Chattanooga, TN.
  • Ambulnz by DocGo will provide vital services in the Chattanooga region, including transportation for hospital discharges and transfers among skilled nursing, rehabilitation and acute care facilities.
  • The Tennessee partnership supports approximately 17,000 annual trips, with plans to exceed 20,000 trips per year in 2025. This expansion is projected to add 5,000 additional trips in the state by 2026.
  • In the past year alone, the company increased the number of trips in Tennessee over 25%, reflecting a growing demand for these services.
  • DocGo (NASDAQ:DCGO) shares were up 2.32% in pre-market trading.

data.title: PHX Energy appoints new CEO
data.description:
• PHX Energy Services (TSX:PHX:CA) announced that John Hooks, the founder and current Chief Executive Officer will be transitioning to Executive Board Chair effective March 1, 2025, and its Board of Directors has appointed, Michael Buker, currently the President, as his CEO successor.
  • In addition to assuming the role of President and CEO, Buker is expected to be nominated for election to the Board of Directors at the Corporation’s next Annual General Meeting of Shareholders.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Shoppers spent less at Etsy during holidays
data.description:
Shoppers spent less at Etsy (NASDAQ:ETSY) during the holiday season, driving down gross merchandise sales (“GMS”) and leaving Wall Street disappointed with the online marketplace’s fourth quarter results and expectations for the current quarter.

Shares were down 5% ahead of Wednesday’s opening bell.

“Despite facing significant GMS headwinds in 2024, we are proud to have delivered year-over-year revenue growth and very strong profitability, while simultaneously investing in our future," said CEO Josh Silverman.

Although the company reactivated 9.8M lapsed buyers from last year, spending on the platform was down 6.8% overall and the number of active buyers fell 2.6%. Spending per buyer was also down, dropping 3.5% from last year to $121, while the number of habitual buyers fell by 9.5% to 6.4M.

But while marketplace revenue was down 1.4%, services revenue increased 8.1%, driving the company’s total revenue to $852.2B, a 1.2% increase from last year but $9.2M less than expected. Profits also improved thanks to a 15% improvement in cost of sales and beat expectations by 9 cents.

Looking ahead to the current quarter, Etsy (NASDAQ:ETSY) continues to anticipate anemic GMS, declining “at a rate similar to the year-over-year performance for the fourth quarter.”
